question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the adjustUpperRange() method which sets secondary bound's upper=lower when the secondary bound's lower has EQ operator, causing the secondary bound to have identical lower and upper references pointing to the same RangeParameter object, and if this object is subsequently mutated by adjustLowerRange(), the upper bound is also silently mutated, producing an incorrect SQL condition that returns unintended allowance records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the removeRanges() method in JooqRepository which creates new Bound objects via toLower() and toUpper() with primarySortField=false, bypassing the range validation in the Bound constructor, and then supply a tokenIds bound where the lower value is greater than the upper value, causing the recursive getBoundConditions() to generate a SQL condition that matches an unintended set of allowance records?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that entityService.lookup() for EntityIdNumParameter directly returns the numeric ID without any database validation (Optional.of(p.id()) without checking if the entity exists), allowing the attacker to query allowances for non-existent or deleted account IDs, potentially returning stale allowance records from the mirror node that no longer reflect the current on-chain state, misleading downstream consumers about valid NFT transfer rights?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mirror node's eventual consistency model — where getNftAllowances() queries a read replica that may lag behind the consensus state — to observe a window where an NFT allowance appears active in the mirror node but has already been revoked on-chain, and use this stale allowance information to trick a third-party application into authorizing an NFT transfer that should be rejected?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SORT_ORDERS map in NftAllowanceRepositoryCustomImpl which uses OrderSpec(byOwner, direction) as a key, and by manipulating the isOwner flag in NftAllowanceRequest, cause the query to sort by SPENDER instead of OWNER (or vice versa), producing a result set that is ordered differently than expected and causing a pagination-based consumer to miss or duplicate allowance records, leading to incorrect allowance state assessment?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any rate limiting or authentication in getNftAllowances() to perform a high-volume enumeration attack — iterating through all entity IDs as accountId with isOwner=true and isOwner=false — to build a complete database of all NFT approvedForAll allowances across the entire Hedera network, enabling systematic identification of high-value targets for NFT theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the payerAccountId field in AbstractNftAllowance — which is exposed in the NftAllowance response via the mapper — to discover which account pays transaction fees for NFT transfers, enabling the attacker to identify fee-payer accounts and target them for social engineering or to predict transaction patterns that reveal high-value NFT movements?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestampRange field in AbstractNftAllowance — exposed in the NftAllowance response — to determine exactly when an NFT allowance was granted or modified, enabling the attacker to correlate allowance creation timestamps with on-chain transaction history to identify the specific transactions that granted approvedForAll rights and reconstruct the victim's NFT management patterns?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NftAllowanceMapper which maps timestampRange to timestamp in the response, combined with the full owner, spender, and tokenId fields in AbstractNftAllowance, to reconstruct a complete picture of all NFT delegation relationships for any account, enabling targeted phishing or social engineering attacks that lead to direct NFT theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNftAllowances() returns the full NftAllowance domain object including owner, spender, tokenId, approvedForAll, and payerAccountId fields without any field-level access control, to harvest sensitive financial relationship data for any account in the system, directly enabling targeted NFT theft by identifying which spenders have unlimited transfer rights?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of isOwner=false and a wide ownerOrSpenderIds range (e.g., GTE 0) to retrieve all NFT allowances where any account is a spender, effectively dumping all approvedForAll grants in the system for a given account, revealing the complete set of NFT collections that account can drain?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdAliasParameter path in entityService.lookup() — which calls entityRepository.findByAlias(p.alias()) — by supplying a crafted alias that matches multiple entities due to alias collision or encoding ambiguity, causing lookup() to return an unexpected entity ID and getNftAllowances() to return allowances for a different account than the one the attacker specified?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdEvmAddressParameter path in entityService.lookup() — which calls entityRepository.findByEvmAddress(p.evmAddress()) — by supplying a crafted EVM address that is a valid hex string but maps to a different entity than expected due to case-insensitive matching or leading-zero handling, causing getNftAllowances() to return allowances for an unintended account?
